alignments.json_text: Senior citizens are prime targets for several kinds of fraud.
alignments.wiki_text: '[delay/echo effect]' Senior citizens are prime targets for several kinds of fraud. Here are a few to watch out for. After reading about a recent death, the obituary con-artist visits the spouse of the deceased person and-- '[beep]' --a convincing story about a debt incurred by the deceased, often to purchase a gift for the survivor. Touched by the deceased person's thoughtfulness, the survivor often pays the bills. The con artist then either disappears with the money, '[beep]' or produces a gift that is greatly over-priced.

alignments.json_text: Using carefully prepared threats, swift phone solicitors try to entice businesses

alignments.json_text: into buying almost anything under the sun.

alignments.json_text: Common sales pitches include advertising space and non-existent publications.
alignments.wiki_text: '[delay/echo effect]' Using carefully prepared scripts, sick phone solicitors try to entice [unintelligible, heavily delayed]-- '[beep]' --anything under the sun. Common sales pitches include: advertising space in non-existent publications, office supplies, and specialty advertising items like calendars, or personalized pens. Some enticers are used-- '[beep]' --in order to make the deals more attractive, and both the sales item and prize are delivered COD. So be careful. Don't buy from these suppliers until you have verified their existence and reliability.
